title: Make task_control operation_id idempotent
goal: Implement a bounded issue #13 idempotency slice so explicit operation_id deduplicates task_control retries, preventing duplicate wake/start/resume side effects such as repeated soft interrupts or task starts.
requiredInputs: Existing swarm mutation state TaskControl and AssignTask response support
Current CommTaskControl does not accept a request_nonce even though the swarm tool exposes operation_id globally
permissions: Modify protocol, swarm tool, task control server handler, and focused tests
Run cargo fmt/check/test and selfdev build
completionConditions: CommTaskControl accepts an optional request_nonce propagated from swarm operation_id for explicit retry idempotency.
When wake/start/resume task_control is called with request_nonce, duplicate calls replay the original TaskControl response instead of repeating side effects.
Task control behavior without operation_id remains unchanged so intentional repeated control calls still execute normally.
Existing retry/reassign/replace/salvage assign_task-backed control paths remain compatible with mutation replay.
Focused protocol/server/tool tests, cargo fmt/check, git diff --check, selfdev build/reload, governance, commit, and push pass.
failureTaxonomy: Changing default task_control behavior without operation_id
Persisting a task_control final response before side effects finish
Over-expanding into full run_plan or task state persistence redesign
notes: Keep the slice to CommTaskControl explicit request_nonce support and operation_id wiring. Prove duplicate wake does not queue duplicate soft interrupts.
